mnemonics: D-P-P-P: The Four Types of Analytics (Descriptive, Predictive, Prescriptive, Diagnostic)
Look Back (Descriptive/Diagnostic), Look Ahead (Predictive), Look Forward with Advice (Prescriptive)
keyPoints: Descriptive Analytics: Summarizes past data to understand what has happened (e.g., reports, dashboards).
Diagnostic Analytics: Analyzes past data to determine why something happened (e.g., drill-down, root cause analysis).
Predictive Analytics: Uses historical data to make forecasts about future outcomes (e.g., forecasting, statistical models).
Prescriptive Analytics: Recommends actions you can take to affect desired outcomes (e.g., optimization, simulation).
commonMistakes: Confusing Predictive and Prescriptive analytics; Predictive forecasts what *will* happen, Prescriptive suggests what you *should* do.
Overlooking Diagnostic analytics or grouping it with Descriptive, even though its purpose (finding cause) is distinct from simply describing events.
Assuming these types are sequential steps; they are often used iteratively and complement each other, not strictly in a fixed order.
